routes.label: Recommended Route
routes.title: By Air via Tugdan Airport
routes.subtitle: Fastest option — ideal for visitors from Manila or Cebu

steps.label: Step 1 — Manila
steps.name: NAIA Terminal → Tugdan Airport (Tablas)
steps.description: Fly directly to Tugdan Airport (TBH) on Tablas Island. Several carriers operate this route; flight time is approximately 55 minutes. Book in advance — seats fill quickly during peak season and holidays.

steps.label: Step 2 — Tugdan Airport
steps.name: Airport → San Agustin Terminal (Jeepney or Tricycle)
steps.description: Upon arrival at Tugdan, take a jeepney or tricycle towards San Agustin, the main commercial hub of Tablas. This is a short ride along the main road.

steps.label: Step 3 — San Agustin
steps.name: San Agustin → Calatrava (Jeepney)
steps.description: Board a jeepney bound for Calatrava from the San Agustin terminal. The route passes through several barangays along Tablas' northern coast and offers scenic views along the way. Jeepneys depart when full.

steps.label: Arrival
steps.name: Calatrava Poblacion
steps.description: You arrive at the town center of Calatrava. From here, tricycles take you to any barangay or resort. Contact your accommodation in advance to arrange a pick-up if needed.

routes.label: Alternative Route
routes.title: By Sea from Batangas
routes.subtitle: Budget-friendly — great for those with time and adventure in mind

steps.label: Step 1 — Metro Manila
steps.name: Manila → Batangas Port (Bus or Car)
steps.description: Head to Batangas Grand Terminal and board a bus to Batangas Port. Several bus lines operate frequent trips from Cubao, Buendia, and other Manila terminals. Alternatively, drive or take a private vehicle.

steps.label: Step 2 — Batangas Port
steps.name: Ferry to Odiongan or Romblon Town
steps.description: RORO ferries depart Batangas Port bound for Odiongan (Tablas) or Romblon Town. Check the latest schedules with Montenegro Shipping Lines or other operators. Overnight trips are common and save on accommodation costs.

steps.label: Step 3 — Odiongan
steps.name: Odiongan Port → Calatrava (Jeepney)
steps.description: Upon docking at Odiongan, board a jeepney heading north toward Santa Fe or Calatrava. The road runs along Tablas Island's interior and coastline. The ride is scenic and comfortable.

steps.label: Arrival
steps.name: Calatrava Poblacion
steps.description: Arrive at the Calatrava town center, rested and ready to explore. Tricycles are available at the terminal for the final leg to your accommodation or destination.

infoBoxes.title: Local Transport in Calatrava
infoBoxes.intro: Once in Calatrava, tricycles are the primary mode of transport for getting around the poblacion and to nearby barangays.
items: Island hopping boats depart from Calatrava Port Terminal
Contact the Tourism Council to arrange guided transport

infoBoxes.title: Best Time to Visit
infoBoxes.intro: Calatrava is at its finest during the dry season when skies are clear and seas are calm for island hopping.
items: Wet season: June to October — some trails may be inaccessible
Tinagong Dagat and waterfalls are accessible year-round

infoBoxes.title: Good to Know
infoBoxes.intro: A few practical things to prepare before your visit to make the trip smoother.
items: Bring cash — ATMs are limited in Calatrava
Mobile signal: Globe and Smart available; may be spotty in barangays
Entrance fees apply at most destinations (₱20–₱25)
Guides are required and arranged through Tourism Council

tips.title: Book Flights Early
tips.text: Flights to Tugdan Airport from Manila have limited seats and can sell out weeks in advance, especially around Holy Week, Christmas, and summer. Book at least 2–4 weeks ahead during peak season.

tips.title: Call Ahead for Guides
tips.text: Contact the Calatrava Tourism Council (0919 335 0314) before arrival to arrange guides for waterfalls and island hopping. This ensures availability and gives local operators time to prepare.

tips.title: Withdraw Cash in Advance
tips.text: ATM availability is very limited in Calatrava. Withdraw enough cash in Odiongan, San Agustin, or before leaving the mainland. Most resorts, guides, and eateries do not accept cards.

tips.title: Pack Light, Pack Smart
tips.text: If trekking to the waterfalls, wear closed shoes and bring a change of clothes. For island hopping, reef-safe sunscreen and a dry bag are essential. Light, breathable clothing works best year-round.
